title: Fix dashboard data loading, actor availability, habits saving, pricing buttons, and add admin system

requirements.text: Fix the 'actor not available' error on the dashboard and throughout the app. Ensure the backend actor is properly initialized and available before any queries or mutations are executed. Add defensive checks so that queries dependent on the actor do not fire until the actor is ready, and display appropriate loading states instead of errors.

quotes: on the dashboard pages I can access and re-edit but I can't log activity
Fix the 'actor not available' error so activity logging works correctly

acceptanceCriteria: No 'actor not available' error appears on the dashboard after login
All React Query hooks in useQueries.ts gate execution on actor availability (enabled flag)
Loading spinners or skeletons are shown while the actor is initializing
Activity logging on the dashboard works successfully after login

requirements.text: Fix dashboard data not loading. Ensure that all dashboard data (stats, today's activities, habit progress bars) is fetched and rendered correctly after the actor becomes available. Fix any query keys, stale data, or missing invalidation that prevents data from appearing on the DashboardPage.

quotes: one more thing in dashboard data isn't loading so fix it too

acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard stats (hours, earnings, productivity) load and display correctly
Today's activity timeline renders fetched activities
Habit progress bars display current progress from fetched data
Data refreshes correctly when the user logs a new activity or changes the date filter

requirements.text: Fix habit creation on the HabitsPage so that new habits are saved to the backend canister. Ensure the createHabit mutation in useQueries.ts is correctly calling the backend actor method, handling errors gracefully, and invalidating the habits query cache on success so the new habit appears immediately.

quotes: in habits I can't create any habit because it can't save anything

acceptanceCriteria: A new habit submitted via HabitFormModal is saved to the backend canister
The habits list updates immediately after a successful save
An error toast is shown if saving fails
The habit form modal closes after a successful save

requirements.text: Wire up all buttons on the PricingPage so they are fully functional. Plan selection buttons should navigate authenticated users to the PaymentPage with the selected plan as a parameter, and prompt unauthenticated users to log in. Ensure CTAs and any other buttons on the page perform their intended actions without errors.

quotes: Go in the pricing page, every button isn't working

acceptanceCriteria: Clicking a plan button on PricingPage navigates to PaymentPage with the correct plan selected
Unauthenticated users are redirected to LoginPage when clicking a plan button
No console errors occur when any pricing page button is clicked
All CTA buttons on the pricing page respond to click events

requirements.text: Add an admin system to the backend. Store an admin principal list in the canister state. Hardcode the app owner's Internet Identity principal as the initial admin. Expose backend functions: isAdmin(caller) returning Bool, getAdminPrincipal() returning the admin principal, and any admin-only functions (getAllUsers, getPlatformStats, approvePayment, rejectPayment, manageCoupons) gated behind an admin check that traps or returns an error for non-admin callers.

quotes: im the owner add an admin setup
Set up an admin role tied to your Internet Identity principal

acceptanceCriteria: Backend has an admin principals list initialized with the owner's principal
isAdmin query returns true only for the designated admin principal
All admin-only backend functions reject calls from non-admin principals
Admin functions for user management, stats, payments, and coupons are protected

requirements.text: Add frontend admin access control. In useQueries.ts, expose an isAdmin query that checks if the current user is admin via the backend. In the Sidebar, show admin navigation links only when isAdmin is true. Protect the AdminPage route so non-admin users are redirected to the dashboard. Remove or supplement the existing session-storage password gate on AdminPage with the principal-based admin check.

quotes: protected admin-only routes and the ability to manage users, view all data, and access restricted dashboard features

acceptanceCriteria: Admin navigation links in the Sidebar are visible only to the admin user
Non-admin users who navigate to /admin are redirected to /dashboard
AdminPage loads correctly for the admin user without the password gate blocking them
isAdmin state is derived from the backend principal check, not hardcoded on the frontend

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
All Motoko logic must remain in the single backend/main.mo actor file
Admin principal must be set via a real Internet Identity principal — do not use a placeholder
nonGoals: Adding a separate admin backend service or microservice
Multi-admin role management beyond owner-level admin
